The parser test module already asserts full AST behavior in `src/odata_parse_tests.rs`, which is the right place for a minimal one-character identifier regression. I’m adding only that test.
I’m going to patch `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs` with one behavior-level test: `x eq 1` must parse into an equality comparison on identifier `x` and numeric value `1`.
The reproducer is in place. I’m checking the package name and then running the single deterministic cargo test command that should be RED on this tree.
The package is `cf-modkit-odata`; the test file is already part of the crate’s lib tests. I’m running the focused RED command now.

item.text: The focused command fails for the expected reason: the parser rejects `x` at column 2 before it can parse `eq 1`. I’m collecting the final file/status details for the structured artifact.

arguments.summary_title: bug6 verified: OData parser rejects single-character identifiers
arguments.summary_markdown:
## What is broken
`cf-modkit-odata` rejects a valid OData filter when the field identifier is a single character. The new regression test asserts that `parse_str("x eq 1")` succeeds and yields an equality comparison between identifier `x` and numeric value `1`.

## Reproduction
I added `odata_parse_tests::single_character_identifier` in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs` and ran the focused lib test command:

```sh
cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier
```

The test is RED on the current tree. It panics because `parse_str("x eq 1")` returns `ParseError::Parsing` at column 2 instead of an AST.

## Evidence
- Test file: `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs`
- Failing command output includes: `single-character identifiers are valid: Parsing("error at 1:2: expected ['a'..='z'|'A'..='Z'|'_'|'0'..='9']")`

## Implicated services/components
- `cf-modkit-odata` parser implementation in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs`, specifically the PEG `identifier` rule requiring one or more trailing identifier characters after the first character.
- `cf-modkit-odata` filter parse API exposed through `odata_filters::parse_str`, which surfaces the parse failure to callers.
steps: Add the regression test `odata_parse_tests::single_character_identifier` to `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs`, asserting that `parse_str("x eq 1")` returns `Expr::Compare(Identifier("x"), Equal, Number(1))`.
Observe that the test fails on the current tree because `parse_str("x eq 1")` returns a parse error instead of the expected AST.
arguments.expected_outcome: `parse_str("x eq 1")` succeeds and returns an equality comparison whose left side is identifier `x` and right side is numeric value `1`.
arguments.actual_outcome: `cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier` exits 101. The test fails with `single-character identifiers are valid: Parsing("error at 1:2: expected ['a'..='z'|'A'..='Z'|'_'|'0'..='9']")`.

environment_check.notes: No external services are required; this is a deterministic Rust lib test.

involved_components.name: cf-modkit-odata
involved_components.reason: The crate owns the OData filter parser and exposes the `odata_filters::parse_str` API that fails for `x eq 1`.

involved_components.name: libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse.rs
involved_components.reason: The PEG `identifier` rule currently requires at least one trailing identifier character after the first character, rejecting one-character identifiers.

involved_components.name: libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs
involved_components.reason: This file now contains the focused RED regression test that asserts the correct parser behavior.

item.text:
Submitted the validated reproduction artifact. The RED reproducer is in `libs/modkit-odata/src/odata_parse_tests.rs`, and the gate command is:

```sh
cargo test -p cf-modkit-odata --lib single_character_identifier
```

It fails now with the parser error at `x eq 1`, as required.
